zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset於1426年7月出生在英國薩默塞特登斯特時，他的父親Sir John Luttrell32歲，母親Margaret Touchet28歲。他於1450年1月22日與Elizabeth Courtenay在英國英格蘭戴文波德哈姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1460年登記服兵役。他去世於1461年2月17日在英國賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯享年34歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀福爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset于1426年7月出生在英国薩默塞特登斯特时，他的父亲Sir John Luttrell32岁，母亲Margaret Touchet28岁。他于1450年1月22日与Elizabeth Courtenay在英国英格兰戴文波德哈姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1460年登记服兵役。他去世于1461年2月17日在英国賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯享年34岁，埋葬在英国英格兰賀福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Luttrell,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): from a diminutive of Old French loutre ‘otter’ (from Latin lutra), applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble an otter, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who hunted otters (for their pelts). Compare Luter and Litteral .

variants.name: Luttrull
meanings: English: variant of   Luttrell  .

variants.name: Littrell
meanings: English: variant of   Luttrell  .

variants.name: Littrel
meanings: English: variant of   Luttrell  .

variants.name: Litteral
meanings: Altered form of English   Luttrell  .

variants.name: Luter
meanings: from Old French loutre ‘otter’, either used as a nickname, or alternatively as a metonymic occupational name to denote an otter hunter.
variant of   Luther   2.
English:

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Dunster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Dunster in Somerset, recorded in 1138 as Dunestore, from the Old English personal name Dun(n) + torr ‘craggy pinnacle’.
History: Henry Dunster emigrated to MA in 1640 from Bury, Lancashire, England, and was made the first president of Harvard College (1640–54) almost immediately upon arrival in MA.

nameMeanings.name: Castle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English castel ‘castle, fortified building or set of buildings’, especially the residence of a feudal lord (from Late Latin castellum, a diminutive of castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’). The name would also have denoted a servant who lived and worked at such a place, or someone who had an obligation to provide services or rent to a castle.

variants.name: Castell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Roberto, Amarilys, Carlos, Eduardo, Estevan, Francisco, Guadalupe, Humberto, Jorge, Manuel, Mateo, Pablo.
English and Scottish: variant of   Castle  .

Manx: shortened form of Gaelic Mac Asgaill ‘son of Ásketill’, an Old Norse personal name composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’. The Manx surname is cognate with Scottish Gaelic McAskill .

variants.name: Cassell
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name of Norman origin, from Cassel in the Nord department, Flanders, France.
English: sometimes a variant of   Castle  .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Caisile, which is probably in origin a variant of Ó Caiside, see Cassidy .

variants.name: Castel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Benoit, Herve, Jean Michel, Thierry, Yves.  Spanish Jose, Blanco, Carlos, Rafael.
English: variant of   Castle  .
Aragonese, Catalan, and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Castell   3, a cognate of 5 below.

variants.name: Castles
meanings: English: variant of Castle , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Armagh and Meath): in Ireland, this may be the English name, but it may also be an Anglicized form of Ó Caisil; see Cashell .

nameMeanings.name: in
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Kyung, Sung, Chan, Duk, Eun Suk, Hee, Hyun, Hyung, Ik, Jae, Kon, Kwang Won, Chang, Chung, Chong, Hyun Jin, Inki, Jeong, Jin Tae, Joo, Myong, Ok Soon, Poon, Poong.  Southeast Asian Hung, Khen, My, Phan, Tek, Thang, Tok.

Korean: name of a family that migrated from China to Korea during the reign of Shilla's 3rd-century king Sok Yu-rye (284–298). The founding ancestor, In So, established a clan seat in the Kyodong area of Cholla North Province. Another clan seat was subsequently established by his descendent, In Pin, during the Koryo period. The surname is not common in Korea.
Cambodian: written អ៊ិន, unexplained.

variants.name: Yin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Feng, Hong, Jian, Kong, Li, Man, Min, Ning, Yuan, Bei, Chao, Ching, Chong, Chung, Heng, Yeng, Tun, Youn.  Cambodian Hai, Phal, Phon, Pu, Samnang, Tho, Thy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 殷, meaning ‘plentiful’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Yin (殷), the name of the capital (located in present-day Henan province) of the Shang dynasty (1600–1046 BC ). Pan Geng, the 19th king of the Shang dynasty, moved the original capital Yan (located in present-day Shandong province) to Yin. Thus the Shang dynasty was also known as the Yin dynasty. After the Shang dynasty was overthrown by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ), people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from Yin Shui (殷水, ‘River Yin’), the name of a river in Shangshui in Henan Province. People who lived along the river acquired 殷 (Yin), the name of the river, as their surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 尹, a term for the official title in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Yin (尹), the name of a fief (said to have been located in Shexian in Shanxi province or Xin’an in Henan province), which was granted to Yin, the son of the legendary king Shao Hao (c. 26th century BC ). (ii) from the personal name of Shi Yin (師尹), literally meaning ‘head of officers’, the name of an official post during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771 BC ). (iii) from the second element of Ling Yin (令尹), the name of an official post in charge of the military in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). This post was held by a prince of the state of Shen called Cheng, who migrated to the state of Chu when he took up the post.

nameMeanings.name: Somerset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

habitational name from the English county of Somerset, which takes its name from Somerton in Somerset (see Summerton ) + Old English sǣte ‘settlers, dwellers’. The Somerset family who are Dukes of Beaufort are descended from the Plantagenets. The surname was assumed by Charles Somerset (1460–1520), illegitimate son of Henry Beaufort, Duke of Somerset. The latter was himself descended illegitimately from Edward III through John of Gaunt. A later member of the family was Fitzroy Somerset (1788–1855), who, as Lord Raglan, was the commander of British forces in the Crimea, one of the men responsible for the charge of the Light Brigade.

possibly a habitational name from one or more places called with Old Norse sumar ‘summer’ + sætr ‘shieling, hill pasture’, such as Summerseat in Elton (Lancashire), but decisive northern early bearers are lacking.

variants.name: Summerton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Somerton, particularly in Oxfordshire and Somerset, but also in Lincolnshire and Norfolk, named with Old English sumor ‘summer’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Summersett
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Somerset  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset was born in July 1426, in Dunster, Somerset, England, his father, Sir John Luttrell, was 32 and his mother, Margaret Touchet, was 28. He married Elizabeth Courtenay on 22 January 1450, in Powderham, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He registered for military service in 1460. He died on 17 February 1461, in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 34, and was buried in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset est né en juillet 1426, à Dunster, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Luttrell, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Margaret Touchet, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Courtenay le 22 janvier 1450, à Powderham, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1460. Il est décédé le 17 février 1461, à St Albans, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterré à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset im Juli 1426 in Dunster, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Luttrell 32 und seine Mutter Margaret Touchet 28. Er hat am 22. Januar 1450 in Powderham, Devon, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Courtenay geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er hat sich 1460 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 17. Februar 1461 im Alter von 34 Jahren in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England und wurde in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset è nato il nel luglio 1426 nel Dunster, Somerset, England, il suo padre, Sir John Luttrell, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Touchet, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Courtenay il 22 gennaio 1450, nel Powderham, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1460. Morì il 17 febbraio 1461, nel St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolto a Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somersetが1426年7月にイギリスソメルセトダンスターで生まれた時，父Sir John Luttrellは32歳，母Margaret Touchetは28歳であった。1450年1月22日にイギリスイギリスデボンポウデルハムでElizabeth Courtenayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1460年に兵役登録をした。彼は1461年2月17日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスト アルバンスで34歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset(이)가 1426년 7월 영국 서머세트 던스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Luttrell(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Touchet(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1450년 1월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 파우더함에서 Elizabeth Courtenay(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1460년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1461년 2월 17일 영국 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset nasceu em julho de 1426, em Dunster, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Luttrell, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Touchet, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Courtenay em 22 de janeiro de 1450, em Powderham, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1460. Ele faleceu em 17 de fevereiro de 1461, em St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, com 34 anos, e foi sepultado em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset родился июль 1426 г. в местности дунстир, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Luttrell было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Touchet, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Courtenay 22 января 1450 г. в местности поудирам, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. В 1460 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 17 февраля 1461 г. в местности ст албанс, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 34 лет и был похоронен в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir James Luttrell, of Dunster Castle in Somerset nació en julio de 1426, en Dunster, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Luttrell, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Margaret Touchet, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Courtenay el 22 de enero de 1450, en Powderham, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1460. Murió el 17 de febrero de 1461, en St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultado en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
